text.title: multi-agent-conversational Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for multi-agent-conversational as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Echo chambers: agents trained on similar data converge on the same (possibly wrong) answer even after many turns of apparent disagreement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Conversational diversity is only real if agents have genuinely different system prompts that frame the problem differently. Identical or near-identical system prompts produce cosmetic disagreement that resolves to the same prior. Assign clearly opposing roles (proponent vs opponent, critic vs advocate) to force genuine divergence.

failure-mode.description: Passing full conversation history to every turn prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Turn N sees N-1 prior messages, causing quadratic token growth. A 10-turn conversation with 200-token turns averages 1000 tokens of context per call instead of 600 with a 3-turn window — cost nearly doubles for no quality gain.
